data.title: airSlate SignNow Tutorial: Sign & Automate Docs Fast
data.excerpt: Master airSlate SignNow in 5 easy steps. Learn how to send, sign, and automate documents without paper. Start your free trial today and speed up your workflow.

$media.alt: airSlate SignNow Tutorial: Sign & Automate Docs Fast

children.text: If you have ever lost a day chasing signatures on a PDF — printing, scanning, emailing back and forth — you already know why electronic signature tools exist. But not all of them actually simplify your life. Some just move the chaos from paper to screen. That is exactly where
airSlate SignNow

stands out. It is not just a signature tool. It is a full document workflow platform that lets you build forms, route contracts, collect payments, and automate repetitive paperwork — all from one dashboard.

I have been using SignNow for client onboarding, vendor agreements, and internal approvals. The speed difference is real. What used to take three days of follow-up emails now takes about four minutes. In this step-by-step tutorial, I will walk you through setting up your first document workflow so you can experience the same thing.
*Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*
What Makes airSlate SignNow Different
Before we get into the steps, let me explain why
I specifically recommend SignNow over other eSignature tools
. Plenty of apps can collect a signature. SignNow goes further:
Unlimited users on every plan.
You do not pay per seat. Your entire team can use it without the bill exploding.
Built-in document generation.
Pull data from other apps to auto-populate contracts.
Advanced workflow automation.
Set approval chains, conditional routing, and reminders — no coding needed.
Deep integrations.
Works with Salesforce, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, HubSpot, and hundreds more through Zapier.
Mobile-first design.
The iOS and Android apps are genuinely usable, not afterthoughts.

The platform holds a 4.6 out of 5 rating on Capterra with over 530 reviews, and it earned a spot on their 2026 Shortlist for eSignature software. That kind of consistent user satisfaction does not happen by accident.
If you want a deeper overview of the platform itself, check out
what airSlate SignNow is and how it works
Step 1: Create Your Account and Pick a Plan
Head to
signNow
and click
Get Started
. You will see a free trial option that gives you full access to the Business plan features — no credit card required.
Here is a quick breakdown of the pricing tiers:
| Plan | Price | Best For |
| Business | $10/month (billed annually) | Individuals and small teams needing core eSignature features |
| Business Premium | Higher tier | Teams that need signing orders, bulk sends, and advanced fields |
| Enterprise | Custom | Organizations requiring API access, advanced integrations, and compliance features |
| airSlate Business Cloud | Custom | Full document workflow automation beyond just signatures |

The Business plan at $10 per month is remarkable value. For context, DocuSign's comparable plan starts at $10 per month but charges per user. With SignNow, you add your whole team for that same price. That alone saved my team over $400 a year.

Once you are in, the dashboard greets you with a clean interface. No clutter. The left sidebar shows your documents, templates, teams, and settings. You will not need a manual to find your way around.
Step 2: Upload or Build Your First Document
Click the
New Document
button. You have three options:
Upload a file
— drag and drop a PDF, Word doc, or image.
Create from a template
— use one of SignNow's pre-built templates or your own saved ones.
Generate from scratch
— use the built-in document builder with text fields, tables, and conditional logic.
For this tutorial, upload an existing PDF. I used a standard consulting agreement. The document opens in the editor, and you will see a toolbar at the top with field types:
Signature
Initials
Date
Text
Checkbox
Dropdown
Attachment request
Payment collection
Formula fields
Drag the
Signature
field onto the document where you need it. Resize it by pulling the corner. Click on the field to set properties: is it required? Who should fill it? Should it lock after signing?

The drag-and-drop editor feels intuitive. I have used tools where placing fields felt like solving a puzzle. Here, it just works. Fields snap to alignment guides, and you can preview the signer experience at any time.
For more advanced document creation techniques, I wrote a detailed
tips and best practices guide
that covers conditional logic and template optimization.
Step 3: Add Recipients and Set the Signing Order
Click
Invite to Sign
. Add your recipients by email. Now here is where SignNow's workflow power shines:
Signing order.
Drag recipients into sequence. Person A signs first, then it automatically routes to Person B. No manual forwarding.
Role-based fields.
Assign specific fields to specific people. The client only sees their signature box; your manager only sees the approval checkbox.
CC notifications.
Keep stakeholders in the loop without giving them signing authority.
Expiration dates.
Set a deadline. SignNow sends automatic reminders as the date approaches.

I set up a three-person signing chain: me (the sender), my client, and our legal reviewer. Each person gets their own personalized view of the document. The client does not see internal notes. Legal does not see pricing fields. Clean separation, zero confusion.

You can also add a custom message to the signing invitation email. I always include a one-line summary of what they are signing and why it matters. Personal touches increase completion rates — my open-to-sign time dropped by 60% after I started customizing those emails.
Ready to try it yourself?
Start your free SignNow trial here
and set up your first multi-signer workflow in minutes.
Step 4: Send, Track, and Manage Signatures
Hit
Send
. That is it. SignNow dispatches personalized signing links to each recipient in the order you defined.
Switch to the
Dashboard
to track everything in real time:
Pending
— who has not opened or signed yet.
In Progress
— who is currently reviewing.
Completed
— fully executed documents.
Declined
— with optional decline reasons.

The status updates are instantaneous. When my client opened the document, I knew within seconds. When they signed, a completed PDF appeared in my inbox automatically, along with a full audit trail: IP address, timestamp, and authentication method.

This audit trail is legally binding under the ESIGN Act and eIDAS regulation. For industries like healthcare, finance, or real estate, that compliance matters. SignNow holds SOC 2 Type II certification and supports HIPAA-compliant workflows.
You can also set up
in-person signing

for situations where someone is sitting next to you. Pull up the document on your tablet, and they sign directly on the screen. I use this during client meetings — it looks professional and eliminates the "I will sign it when I get back to the office" delay.
If you are curious about real-world applications, I have compiled
practical use cases for airSlate SignNow
across different industries.
Step 5: Build Templates and Automate Repetitive Workflows
This is where SignNow transforms from a signing tool into a productivity engine.
After you send a document once, click
Save as Template

. The next time you need to send the same agreement, you load the template, update the variable fields (client name, date, project scope), and send. What took fifteen minutes of setup now takes thirty seconds.
But templates are just the beginning. With the
airSlate Business Cloud
tier, you can:
Auto-generate documents
from CRM data. When a deal closes in Salesforce, a contract pre-filled with client details is created and sent automatically.
Build multi-step approval workflows.
A purchase request goes from requester to manager to finance, each step triggered by the previous approval.
Integrate with 100+ apps
via native connectors and Zapier. Connect SignNow to Slack for notifications, to Google Sheets for data logging, or to Stripe for payment collection on signed contracts.
Use conditional logic.
If a client checks "Yes" on the additional services box, a second page of terms appears automatically. If "No," it skips ahead.

I automated my entire client onboarding flow with this. A new client fills out an intake form on our website. That triggers a welcome packet, an NDA, and a service agreement — all pre-populated, all routed to the right people, all tracked in one place. My team went from spending four hours per new client to spending fifteen minutes reviewing auto-generated documents.
For a full list of the
latest features and updates
, check our dedicated breakdown.
And if you are managing multiple tools in your stack, I also wrote guides on
how to use ClickUp
monday.com
, and
Apollo.io
— all of which pair beautifully with SignNow for end-to-end workflow management.
Who Is airSlate SignNow Ideal For
SignNow is versatile enough to serve a wide range of users, but it truly excels for:
Small and mid-size businesses
that need professional-grade eSignatures without enterprise-grade pricing. The unlimited-user model is a game-changer for growing teams.
Freelancers and solopreneurs
sending contracts, proposals, and invoices. The mobile app means you can get a signature from a coffee shop or a client site.
Sales teams
that need fast turnaround on proposals and agreements. The Salesforce and HubSpot integrations let reps send contracts without leaving their CRM.
HR departments
managing offer letters, policy acknowledgments, and onboarding packets at scale. Bulk send and template features handle high-volume document needs effortlessly.
Legal and compliance teams
requiring detailed audit trails, tamper-proof documents, and regulatory adherence. The SOC 2 and HIPAA certifications cover strict requirements.
Real estate agents and property managers
dealing with leases, disclosures, and purchase agreements. In-person signing and mobile-first design match their on-the-go workflow.
Basically, if your work involves getting documents signed, reviewed, or approved — and you want it done faster with less manual effort — SignNow fits.
Ready to Ditch the Paper Chase?
I have tried a lot of document tools. Some are bloated. Some are overpriced. Some are simple but lack the automation layer that actually saves time.
airSlate SignNow
hits the sweet spot: powerful enough for complex workflows, simple enough that your least tech-savvy colleague can sign without calling IT.
The pricing is honest. The features are real. And the unlimited-user model means you will not get punished for growing your team.

Start with the free trial. Send your first document. Watch how fast it comes back signed. That moment — when a contract lands in your inbox completed in four minutes instead of four days — is when you will understand why I keep recommending this tool.

Frequently Asked Questions
Is airSlate SignNow legally binding?

Yes. SignNow complies with the U.S. ESIGN Act, UETA, and the European eIDAS regulation. Every signed document includes a tamper-evident audit trail with timestamps, IP addresses, and authentication details. Courts and regulatory bodies accept these signatures as legally valid.
Can I use SignNow on my phone?

Absolutely. SignNow has fully functional iOS and Android apps. You can upload documents, add fields, send signing requests, and track status from your phone. In-person signing on a tablet is also supported, which is perfect for client meetings or field work.
How does SignNow pricing compare to DocuSign or Adobe Sign?

SignNow's standout advantage is unlimited users on every plan. Competitors like DocuSign and Adobe Sign charge per user, which gets expensive fast for teams. At $10 per month billed annually for the Business plan, SignNow delivers strong value — especially when you factor in the workflow automation features included at that price point.
Does SignNow integrate with my existing tools?

SignNow offers native integrations with Salesforce, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, HubSpot, SharePoint, and many more. Through Zapier and its open API, you can connect it to virtually any app in your stack, including CRMs, project management tools, and accounting software.
What happens to my documents after they are signed?

Completed documents are stored securely in your SignNow account with full encryption. You can download them as PDFs, sync them to cloud storage like Google Drive or Dropbox, or set up automatic filing rules. The audit trail stays attached to each document permanently.
